purpose: Dedicated Epstein Evidence Watch for Matrix Reprogrammed: official-source-first document tracking, dated bulletins, PDF/source routing, evidence boundaries, Rumble/video routes, opt-ins, offers, and books.
boundary: This system tracks public records, source pages, court/declassification routes, news bulletins, archive links, committee activity, and litigation movement. A named person appearing in a public record, flight log, contact book, civil filing, news article, testimony, or document set is not by itself proof of criminal conduct.
rules: Official sources, court records, and primary documents outrank commentary.
Every bulletin must be dated and linked to a source route.
Every named association must preserve the association boundary.
The page should sell books and routes without inflating evidence strength.
PDF/source links should be treated as evidence doors, not as automatic claims.
Court petitions and testimony are claims or process records until a court, jury, agency, or committee finding says otherwise.
If a live source cannot be checked during build, the site should still build and show the last configured route.

watchSources.title: Court Records And Civil Filings
watchSources.use: Track court filings, habeas petitions, rulings, depositions, docket movement, and source documents.

watchSources.title: Maxwell Litigation / Habeas Lane
watchSources.use: Track new legal challenges, prosecution responses, judicial rulings, and claims about document releases without treating filings as findings.

watchSources.title: DOJ / FBI Public Record Lane
watchSources.use: Track official public statements, search pages, releases, redactions, disclosures, and document references.

watchSources.title: Congressional Oversight Lane
watchSources.use: Track committee statements, interviews, letters, source releases, subpoenas, and public oversight movement.

watchSources.title: News Bulletin Lane
watchSources.use: Track current reporting, new releases, corrections, litigation summaries, and dated news items.

watchSources.title: Archive And Research Lane
watchSources.use: Track public archive mirrors, document sets, PDF collections, and preserved files.

watchSources.title: Rumble / Video Route Lane
watchSources.use: Route document bulletins into explainers, shorts, long-form videos, and Rumble channel pathways.

bulletins.title: Maxwell files new Epstein-documents challenge; prosecutors oppose
bulletins.label: Court-process update
bulletins.summary: Reuters reported that Ghislaine Maxwell filed a new habeas corpus petition arguing that newly released Epstein documents undermine her conviction, while U.S. prosecutors rejected most arguments as untimely or legally baseless. Treat this as litigation movement, not a finding that the claims are true.

bulletins.title: House Oversight testimony lane adds Epstein staff, scheduling, and logistics context
bulletins.label: Oversight update
bulletins.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported on House Oversight testimony from Epstein's longtime assistant Lesley Groff. The public-record value is structural: schedules, logistics, massage appointments, claimed knowledge boundaries, and committee questioning. Testimony must be separated from findings.

bulletins.title: Private settlements and NDAs move into the oversight question
bulletins.label: Evidence-boundary update
bulletins.summary: Business Insider reported that confidential pre-litigation settlements and non-disclosure agreements are part of the congressional oversight debate. Settlements are not automatic admissions of guilt, but they may affect what witnesses can say publicly unless subpoenaed or otherwise released.

bulletins.title: DOJ watchdog compliance review remains a core source lane
bulletins.label: Government oversight
bulletins.summary: AP reported earlier in 2026 that the Justice Department inspector general is reviewing compliance with the law requiring Epstein-file disclosures, including collection, redaction, and disclosure processes. This stays on the watch list because process errors can reshape what the public record actually shows.
